arxiv.title: ChipNeMo: Domain-Adapted LLMs for Chip Design
arxiv.abstract: ChipNeMo aims to explore the applications of large language models (LLMs) for industrial chip design. Instead of directly deploying off-the-shelf commercial or open-source LLMs, we instead adopt the following domain adaptation techniques: domain-adaptive tokenization, domain-adaptive continued pretr

arxiv.title: BadLlama: cheaply removing safety fine-tuning from Llama 2-Chat 13B
arxiv.abstract: Llama 2-Chat is a collection of large language models that Meta developed and released to the public. While Meta fine-tuned Llama 2-Chat to refuse to output harmful content, we hypothesize that public access to model weights enables bad actors to cheaply circumvent Llama 2-Chat's safeguards and weap

arxiv.title: The Alignment Ceiling: Objective Mismatch in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
arxiv.abstract: Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) has emerged as a powerful technique to make large language models (LLMs) more capable in complex settings. RLHF proceeds as collecting human preference data, training a reward model on said data, and optimizing a base ML model with respect to said re

arxiv.title: Object-centric Video Representation for Long-term Action Anticipation
arxiv.abstract: This paper focuses on building object-centric representations for long-term action anticipation in videos. Our key motivation is that objects provide important cues to recognize and predict human-object interactions, especially when the predictions are longer term, as an observed "background" object

arxiv.title: Large-Scale Multi-Robot Assembly Planning for Autonomous Manufacturing
arxiv.abstract: Mobile autonomous robots have the potential to revolutionize manufacturing processes. arxiv.title: Robust Safety Classifier for Large Language Models: Adversarial Prompt Shield
arxiv.abstract: Large Language Models' safety remains a critical concern due to their vulnerability to adversarial attacks, which can prompt these systems to produce harmful responses. In the heart of these systems lies a safety classifier, a computational model trained to discern and mitigate potentially harmful,

arxiv.title: Hierarchical Information-sharing Convolutional Neural Network for the Prediction of Arctic Sea Ice Concentration and Velocity
arxiv.abstract: Forecasting sea ice concentration (SIC) and sea ice velocity (SIV) in the Arctic Ocean is of great significance as the Arctic environment has been changed by the recent warming climate. arxiv.title: Longer Fixations, More Computation: Gaze-Guided Recurrent Neural Networks
arxiv.abstract: Humans read texts at a varying pace, while machine learning models treat each token in the same way in terms of a computational process. Therefore, we ask, does it help to make models act more like humans? In this paper, we convert this intuition into a set of novel models with fixation-guided paral

arxiv.title: Adaptive and non-adaptive minimax rates for weighted Laplacian-eigenmap based nonparametric regression
arxiv.abstract: We show both adaptive and non-adaptive minimax rates of convergence for a family of weighted Laplacian-Eigenmap based nonparametric regression methods, when the true regression function belongs to a Sobolev space and the sampling density is bounded from above and below. arxiv.title: On the effect of curriculum learning with developmental data for grammar acquisition
arxiv.abstract: This work explores the degree to which grammar acquisition is driven by language `simplicity' and the source modality (speech vs. text) of data.
